---
title: "4.2.4 Advantages and disadvantages of compilers and interpreters"
subtitle: "Software P170"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Compilers and Interpreters

### Comparing translator choices

The textbook compares the advantages and disadvantages of <span class="term">compilers</span> and <span class="term">interpreters</span> in Table 4.4.

The comparison focuses on:

::: {.tight-list}
- development and debugging
- whether the translator is needed when the program runs
- memory use
- execution time
:::

## Table 4.4

### Interpreter advantages and disadvantages

::: {.clean-table}
| Interpreter advantages | Interpreter disadvantages |
|---|---|
| Easier and quicker to debug and test programs during development | Programs cannot be run without the interpreter |
| Easier to edit programs during development | Programs can take longer to execute |
:::

## Table 4.4

### Compiler advantages and disadvantages

::: {.clean-table}
| Compiler advantages | Compiler disadvantages |
|---|---|
| A compiled program can be stored ready for use | It takes longer to write, test and debug programs during development |
| A compiled program can be executed without the compiler |  |
| A compiled program takes up less space in memory when executed |  |
| A compiled program is executed in a shorter time |  |
:::

## Classroom Check

### Compare compilers and interpreters

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- interpreters are useful during development because programs are easier to debug, test and edit
- interpreted programs cannot run without the interpreter
- interpreted programs can take longer to execute
- compiled programs can be stored ready for use
- compiled programs can run without the compiler
- compiled programs execute in a shorter time but take longer to write, test and debug
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Integrated Development Environment

The next deck explains how IDE features help programmers write and develop programs.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
